title: make error - undefined: varlinkapi.New
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I'm following [the installation doc](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation) to build podman from scratch for version [2.0.0-rc1](https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/tag/v2.0.0-rc1) on fedora 32. An error happens while doing the make step: `undefined: varlinkapi.New`. I didn't see this when I `make` for previous versions.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. cd go/src/github.com/containers/
2. wget https://github.com/containers/libpod/archive/v2.0.0-rc1.tar.gz
3. tar -xzvf v2.0.0-rc1.tar.gz
4. cd libpod-2.0.0-rc1/
5. export GOPATH=~/go
6. make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@localhost libpod-2.0.0-rc1]# make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"
make: Warning: File 'Makefile' has modification time 24498 s in the future
mkdir -p "/root/go/src/github.com/containers"
ln -sfn "/root/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod-2.0.0-rc1" "/root/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod"
ln -sfn "/root/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod-2.0.0-rc1/vendor/github.com/varlink" "/root/go/src/github.com/varlink"
touch .gopathok
# Only generate the varlink code on Linux (see issue #4814).
GO111MODULE=off go generate ./pkg/varlink/...
fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git
Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag. Install libsystemd on 	Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based distro for journald support.
GO111MODULE=on go build -mod=vendor  -gcflags 'all=-trimpath=/root/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod-2.0.0-rc1' -asmflags 'all=-trimpath=/root/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod-2.0.0-rc1' -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/define.gitCommit= -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/define.buildInfo=1589798152 -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/config._etcDir=/etc ' -tags "ABISupport selinux seccomp" -o bin/podman github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman
# github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/domain/infra/abi
pkg/domain/infra/abi/system.go:37:3: undefined: varlinkapi.New
make: *** [Makefile:184: bin/podman] Error 2
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
make successfully

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
```
# hostnamectl
   Static hostname: localhost.localdomain
         Icon name: computer-vm
           Chassis: vm
        Machine ID: d67f983da189a241a6bfe30d8774b0b2
           Boot ID: efcfae8632fa422dbdca04fc42bca9ef
    Virtualization: kvm
  Operating System: Fedora 32 (Server Edition)
       CPE OS Name: cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:32
            Kernel: Linux 5.6.6-300.fc32.x86_64
      Architecture: x86-64
```

comments.body: hmm, rawhide rpmbuilds work fine with BUILDTAGS  https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/packageinfo?packageID=26289 , but that's because they don't run `make` for building. I can reproduce this issue on master when using BUILDTAGS. Looking...

@Edward5hen can you try `make BUILDTAGS="varlink selinux seccomp"`. The `varlink` tag needs to be specified. 

@baude @jwhonce @vrothberg do we expect this to succeed without the `varlink` tag?

> make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"

I believe we need varlink. `make {binaries, podman, podman-remote}` set the build tags automatically. I recommend use these targets for building.
The `make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"` in the installation docs refers to building runc. Maybe there was a confusion around that?
I would 100% expect Podman to build successfully without Varlink - that's a tag we added ourselves to gate whether Varlink support was added to the build.

> I would 100% expect Podman to build successfully without Varlink - that's a tag we added ourselves to gate whether Varlink support was added to the build.

`# github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/domain/infra/abi` ... I assume the varlink API isn't guarded by the build tag

> > I would 100% expect Podman to build successfully without Varlink - that's a tag we added ourselves to gate whether Varlink support was added to the build.
> 
> `# github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/domain/infra/abi` ... I assume the varlink API isn't guarded by the build tag

thanks, I'll look into this..

> @Edward5hen can you try `make BUILDTAGS="varlink selinux seccomp"`. The `varlink` tag needs to be specified.
> 
> @baude @jwhonce @vrothberg do we expect this to succeed without the `varlink` tag?

@lsm5 this issue is not seen if I add the tag varlink - `make BUILDTAGS="varlink selinux seccomp"`
Yes we need to be able to build without varlink.
FWIW, I cannot reproduce on latest master on F32.
yes, I see that too. I'll update my PR to only get rid of the `go generate` step when building without varlink and not specify varlink tag by default.
